sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Penthrox delivers methoxyflurane — a volatile halogenated analgesic — via a hand-held inhaler for rapid short-term pain relief. Onset of analgesia is within 6–10 breaths. It is self-administered under supervision and is particularly useful in trauma, procedural pain, and during extrication.

sections.title: Procedure
sections.content:
1. Remove Penthrox from packaging; check device is assembled correctly

2. Add 3 mL of methoxyflurane to the inhaler chamber

3. Insert the activated charcoal absorber (black) into the side hole — reduces exhaled waste gas and clinician exposure

4. Instruct patient: hold device, place mouthpiece in mouth, breathe in and out normally

5. Patient controls their own inhalation rate — do not force deep breaths

6. Cover the hole on the side with a finger to increase concentration; uncover for lower concentration

7. Maximum dose per administration: 6 mL per occasion (2 × 3 mL inhalers)

8. Do not use for more than 15 minutes continuously

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Patient self-administration is the safety mechanism — sedation causes patient to drop device
- Ensure adequate ventilation of the space (ambulance windows open)
- Clinician should not inhale exhaled gas — stand clear of patient's exhalation
- Contraindicated: hepatic impairment, renal impairment, malignant hyperthermia susceptibility, MSTeP history
- Onset 6–10 breaths; peak effect 2–3 minutes; wears off rapidly on cessation
